derived.title: Bug Fix Planning
differences: Scope is LOCKED. Do NOT add features. Only fix bugs.
Input is the QA report, not the product spec.
Output is a bug fix plan, not a product plan.
additionalDirectives: SCOPE LOCK: Fix bugs only. Zero feature additions.

sectionOverrides.Your Task:
Read the QA report at `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-06-qa.md` (or the most
recent `phase-10-qa-confirm.md` if this is a repeat fix cycle).

Plan fixes for every bug found. Do NOT add features, improve UX, refactor
code, or do anything that isn't directly fixing a reported bug.
sectionOverrides.Review Process:
### 1. Bug Triage

Read the QA report. List every bug with its classification
(Critical/Major/Minor).

### 2. Root Cause Analysis

For each bug:
- Root cause (one sentence)
- Which file(s) need to change
- What the fix is (one sentence)

### 3. Fix Ordering

Order by severity: Critical first, then Major, then Minor.
sectionOverrides.Output Format:
Write to `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-07-plan-bugfix.md`:

```markdown
# Bug Fix Plan — Run {RUN_ID}

## Bugs to Fix (ordered by severity)

### Critical
1. [bug description]
   - Root cause: [one sentence]
   - Files: [file list]
   - Fix: [one sentence]

### Major
1. [bug description]
   - Root cause: [one sentence]
   - Files: [file list]
   - Fix: [one sentence]

### Minor
1. [bug description]
   - Root cause: [one sentence]
   - Files: [file list]
   - Fix: [one sentence]

## Scope Guard
This plan fixes [N] bugs. It adds 0 features. It refactors 0 things.
```

derived.title: Bug Fix Implementation
differences: Input is the bug fix plan (Phase 07), not the engineering plan.
Only fix bugs. Do NOT add features or refactor.
Do NOT create new files unless absolutely necessary for a fix.
Write a failing test FIRST that reproduces the bug, then fix it.
additionalDirectives: SCOPE LOCK: Fix bugs only. Zero feature additions.

sectionOverrides.Your Task:
Read the bug fix plan at `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-07-plan-bugfix.md`.
Implement every fix using test-first methodology: write a failing test
that reproduces the bug, then make the minimal change to pass it.
sectionOverrides.Process:
1. Read the bug fix plan.
2. For each bug (in severity order):
   a. Write a failing test that reproduces the exact bug scenario.
   b. Verify the test fails (it should — the bug exists).
   c. Make the minimal code change that fixes the bug.
   d. Verify the test now passes.
   e. Run the full suite to check for regressions.
3. After all fixes: run the complete test suite.

## Rules

- **Minimal diff.** Change as few lines as possible per fix.
- **No drive-by improvements.** If you see something ugly near the bug,
  leave it alone.
- **Test first.** Every bug gets a regression test BEFORE the fix.
- **Fix the bug, not the symptom.** Root cause, not band-aid.
sectionOverrides.Completion Log:
Write to `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-08-implement-fix.md`:

```markdown
# Bug Fix Implementation — Run {RUN_ID}

## Fixes Applied
1. [bug]: [file:line] — [what was changed]
   - Regression test: [test file:test name]

## Test Results After Fixes
[paste test output]

## Files Modified
- [file]: [summary]
```

derived.title: Review & Commit Bug Fixes
differences: Only review the bug fix changes, not the entire codebase.
Verify fixes don't introduce new bugs or expand scope.
After review, commit the fixes (merged review + ship phases).
additionalDirectives: SCOPE LOCK: Review and commit bug fixes only.

sectionOverrides.Your Task:
Review the bug fixes from Phase 08. Verify each fix addresses the
reported bug without introducing regressions or scope expansion.
Then commit the fixes.
sectionOverrides.Review Process:
### Pass 1: Fix Verification

Read the fix log at `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-08-implement-fix.md`.
For each fix:
- [ ] Fix addresses the root cause (not just the symptom)
- [ ] Fix is minimal (no unnecessary changes)
- [ ] A regression test exists that reproduces the original bug
- [ ] No new security issues introduced
- [ ] No scope expansion (feature addition, refactoring)

If any fix fails these checks: fix it directly.

### Pass 2: Mechanical Issues (Fix-First)

Scan the modified files for:
- Dead code introduced by the fix — [AUTO-FIXED] remove it
- Stale comments contradicting the fix — [AUTO-FIXED] update them
- Missing error handling around the fix — [AUTO-FIXED] add it

For each mechanical fix: apply it and log as [AUTO-FIXED].

### Pass 3: Commit

1. Run all tests. Fix any regressions.
2. Clean up debug artifacts.
3. Stage and commit:
   ```bash
   git add output/
   git commit -m "fix: [one-line description of bugs fixed]"
   ```
4. Verify clean working tree.
sectionOverrides.Output Format:
Write to `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-09-review-and-commit-fix.md`:

```markdown
# Bug Fix Review & Commit — Run {RUN_ID}

## Fix Verification
1. [bug]: VERIFIED / NEEDS REWORK — [notes]

## Auto-Fixed Issues
- [AUTO-FIXED] [file:line] — [what was done]

## Scope Check
- New features added: 0 (must be 0)
- Files modified outside bug scope: [list or none]

## Commit
- Hash: [short hash]
- Message: [commit message]

## Test Results
[paste test output]
```

derived.title: QA Confirmation
differences: Focus on verifying that previously reported bugs are fixed.
Do NOT expand testing scope beyond original QA findings.
Write regression tests for any bugs confirmed fixed.

sectionOverrides.Your Task:
Verify that all bugs from Phase 06 (or the previous Phase 10) are fixed.
Re-run the success criteria. Report any remaining bugs.
Write regression tests for confirmed fixes.
sectionOverrides.QA Process:
### 1. Bug Verification

Read the most recent QA report:
- `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-06-qa.md` (first fix cycle)
- Or the previous `phase-10-qa-confirm.md` (subsequent cycles)

For each bug that was reported:
- Reproduce the steps from the bug report
- Verify the bug is fixed
- Mark as FIXED or STILL_BROKEN
- If FIXED: write a regression test in `output/tests/regression/{bug-slug}.test.{ext}`
  that reproduces the original bug scenario and verifies it's resolved

### 2. Regression Test Validation

For each regression test written:
- Run it — it must PASS (the bug is fixed)
- Temporarily revert the fix and verify the test FAILS (it catches the bug)
- Re-apply the fix

If no test framework exists, write test stubs with TODO comments.

### 3. Success Criteria Re-Check

Re-verify all success criteria from Phase 01.

### 4. Test Suite

Run the full test suite including new regression tests.

### 5. Quick Regression Sweep

Did the fixes break anything new? One quick exploratory pass.
sectionOverrides.Output Format:
Write to `{PHASE_ARTIFACTS}/phase-10-qa-confirm.md`:

```markdown
# QA Confirmation — Run {RUN_ID}

## Bug Verification
1. [bug]: FIXED / STILL_BROKEN
   - Regression test: [test file] or N/A
2. [bug]: FIXED / STILL_BROKEN

## Regression Tests Written
- output/tests/regression/[slug].test.[ext]: [what it tests]

## Success Criteria Re-Check
1. [criterion]: PASS/FAIL

## Regression Check
- New bugs found: [count or none]
- [details if any]

## Test Suite
- Passed: [n] (including [m] new regression tests)
- Failed: [n]

BUGS_FOUND: [total remaining]
CRITICAL_BUGS: [count]
RECOMMENDATION: FIX_NEEDED / SHIP_READY
```
